rulings.Case Title: TERRA NOVA INDUSTRIES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. M/M WINE REALTY, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion For Leave To Record Lis Pendens Based On Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 20, 2016, line 1. PLAINTIFF TERRA NOVA INDUSTRIES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's Motion For Leave To Record Lis Pendens Based On Third Amended Complaint DENIED. Plaintiff asserts that a claim for specific performance of an alleged oral promise to provide a deed of trust supports a lis pendens. However, the one case plaintiff cites - Hilberg v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 215 Cal. App. 3d 539 - regards a written agreement to actually buy a property. The more-relevant opinions - Urez Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (1987) 190 Cal.App. 3d 1141 and Wardley Development Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1989), 213 Cal. App. 3d 391 - support defendant's position that a lis pendens would be improper in this case. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CITY LIFE PROPERTIES, LLC VS. CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 20, 2016, line 2. DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff states that the question of duty on a negligence cause of action is a legal one for the Court, but its opposition suggests no duty East West Bank could have to it. Plaintiff says its "common counts" claim is "based on" its negligence claim (Opp. 5:11-6:6), so it too fails. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA GRITES VS. MICHELLE FULLER ET AL (Pltf. 170.6 Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 20, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT MICHELLE FULLER's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT DENIED. Plaintiff adequately pleads fraud. Punitive damages are available under multiple counts of the complaint. The motion improperly raises factual disputes. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA GRITES VS. MICHELLE FULLER ET AL (Pltf. 170.6 Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 20, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT MICHELLE FULLER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Plaintiff adequately pleads fraud and intentional infliction of emotional distress. No claim is time-barred, as plaintiff seeks discretionary relief and pleads causes of action beyond wrongful eviction. See Hypertouch, Inc. v. ValueClick, Inc. (2011), 192 Cal. App. 4th 805, 842. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. MARGARET BILLSBOROUGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 20, 2016, line 4. PLAINTIFF JOHN STEWART COMPANY's Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Hearing Required. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES FERGUSON VS. ANNA GATTI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 20, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT ANNA GATTI's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT DENIED. Unlawful detainer is a summary proceeding regarding possession (not an issue in the consolidated civil action). Any applicable affirmative defenses will be addressed on the merits in the unlawful detainer jury trial (if one is requested). = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SUNG CHEE FONG REVOCABLE TRUST VS. JOSE FALCON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff; And To Continue Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 20, 2016, line 6. DEFENDANT JOSE FALCON's Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff; And To Continue Trial Date GRANTED, subject to opposition. = (501/RBU)
